creator: florian.heigl@gmail.com
text:
Hello,

the bug can be closed - the apg binary was broken and instead contained =
some crap.
reinstalled using pkg install apg and i have a sane binary again.

This server isn=92t in backups so I won=92t really be able to trace what =
happened.

Thanks for the support!!!!

On 09.04.2014, at 07:42, Matthew Seaman =
<m.seaman@infracaninophile.co.uk> wrote:

>=20
> Hi,
>=20
> I looks as if apg is somehow reading a date instead of the correct
> input.  Can you run apg under truss(1) and show me the output please?
>=20
>   truss -s 128 -o truss.out apg
>=20
> If you can upload the output to a pastebin site and send me a URL, =
that
> would be great.
>=20
> 	Cheers,
>=20
> 	Matthew
>=20
> --=20
> Dr Matthew J Seaman MA, D.Phil.
>=20
> PGP: http://www.infracaninophile.co.uk/pgpkey
> JID: matthew@infracaninophile.co.uk
>=20
